jobs.title: Operator Visual Inspection 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Stein, AG, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Join our brand new Fill‑Finish facility in Stein as an Operator Visual Inspection, performing manual inspections today while supporting the transition to semi‑ and fully automated processes. This includes qualification runs and routine inspections in a controlled darkroom setting. We’re looking for a motivated and resilient individual who thrives in structured environments, follows GMP guidelines, and contributes to both the project phase and routine production excellence. Check out Your Career in Drug Product  Services at Lonza!

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Stein: https://bit.ly/42DlBDk 

What you will do:

• Manual, semi-automated, and fully automated visual inspection of liquid and lyophilized vials and syringes in a cleanroom setting

• Classification of defects in accordance with regulations

• Regular qualification and requalification using existing test sets

• Inspection of test runs / threshold studies

• Support in deviations and root cause analysis

What we are looking for:

• Strong attention to detail and cleanliness, with an uncompromising approach to quality and safety

• Willing to work in a cleanroom

• Experience in a GMP production environment is highly desirable

• Experience in visual inspection, including relevant further training is a plus

• Completed vocational training (e.g. as a chemical or pharmaceutical technologist) is an advantage

• Fluency in German is a must and basic knowledge of English

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: BIO Operator P1P2 4 Schicht (m/w/d)
jobs.description:
Der tatsächliche Ort dieser Stelle befindet sich in Visp, Schweiz. Berechtigten Bewerbern und ihren Familien steht bei Bedarf Unterstützung beim Umzug zur Verfügung.

Was auf Sie wartet

• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur

• Ein integratives und ethisches Arbeitsumfeld

• Vergütungsprogramme, die hohe Leistung anerkennen

• Eine Vielzahl von Benefits, abhängig von Funktion und Standort

Die vollständige Liste unserer globalen Benefits finden Sie hier:
https://www.lonza.com/careers/benefits

Ihre Aufgaben

• Selbstständiges Fahren der ihm vertrauten Teile der Produktionsanlage gemäss Vorgabedokumenten (Herstellungsprotokolle/ Bedienungsanleitungen / Arbeitsanweisungen / SOP)

• Sichere und richtige Arbeitsausführung unter Einhaltung der Sicherheitsvorschriften unter Berücksichtigung der cGMP Qualitätsanforderungen

• Verantwortung für Sauberkeit und Ordnung am Arbeitsplatz sowie den zugewiesenen Räumen und Lagern

• Erledigen von Überwachungs-, Instandhaltungs-, und Reinigungsarbeiten sowie das Melden und Beheben von Mängeln und Störungen in der Anlage inklusive deren Dokumentation

• Jeder Mitarbeiter / in ist als gleichwertiges Teammitglied durch Seine / Ihre aktive Mithilfe an kontinuierlichen Verbesserungsprozessen  beteiligt

Was wir suchen

• Abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung  vorausgesetzt

• Chemische oder pharmatechnologische Ausbildung sowie GMP und / oder BICO Ausbildung von Vorteil

• Gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort, Schrift und Verständnis

• Akkurate Arbeits- und Dokumentationsweise

• Ausführung der Arbeiten mit hohem Sicherheits- und Qualitätsbewusstsein

• Bereitschaft zur Schichtarbeit ( 4 Schicht-Betrieb) mit Flexibilität bei der Arbeitseinteilung

Über Lonza

Bei Lonza sind unsere Mitarbeitenden unsere grösste Stärke. An über 30 Standorten auf fünf Kontinenten arbeiten unsere global vernetzten Teams täglich zusammen, um die Medikamente von morgen herzustellen.
Unsere Grundwerte – Zusammenarbeit, Verantwortlichkeit, Exzellenz, Leidenschaft und Integrität – spiegeln wider, wer wir sind und wie wir zusammenarbeiten.
Innovation entsteht durch Vielfalt. Deshalb schätzen wir unterschiedliche Perspektiven und fördern ein integratives Arbeitsumfeld für alle Mitarbeitenden. Wenn Sie bereit sind, die Ideen unserer Kunden in wirksame Therapien umzusetzen, freuen wir uns, Sie an Bord begrüssen zu dürfen.

Bereit, die Zukunft der Life Sciences zu gestalten? Jetzt bewerben.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: CQV Engineer / Qualification & Validation Engineer 80-100% (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Join our team at Lonza in Visp, Switzerland as a CQV Engineer / Qualification & Validation Engineer (m/f/d). Work alongside an outstanding team dedicated to maintaining flawless operations and premier standards. This role provides an exciting chance to contribute to our dynamic environment, directly improving the quality and efficiency of our processes. Take part in qualifying and validating brand-new process engineering equipment and facilities, promoting continuous improvement and innovation.

Your Career with Lonza in Visp, Switzerland

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture

• An inclusive and ethical workplace

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance

• In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. The full list of our local tailored benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

What you will do:

• Develop and maintain commissioning and qualification documents for new plants, including PQs for critical systems and ongoing SLC support post-implementation.

• Supervise and resolve deviations, manage changes (GEP and GMP), and implement CAPAs effectively.

• Coordinate efforts between internal teams and external service providers and suppliers to ensure seamless project execution.

• Present qualification documents during audits and inspections, showcasing the outstanding standards maintained.

What we are looking for:

• Graduate from a technical school (HF) / university of applied sciences (FH) or hold a university degree

• Proven experience in a regulated GMP environment, specifically in commissioning, qualification, and validation of systems like refrigerators, freezers, clean media, and process engineering equipment

• Strong proficiency in MS Office; familiarity with COMOS, DMS, Kneat, TrackWise, and SAP is advantageous

• Proficient in both written and spoken German and/or English

• Demonstrate independent, flexible work habits, with excellent communication skills and a structured, focused approach to tasks.

• Open to new ideas, agile in thinking, and proactive in problem-solving.

About Lonza:

jobs.title: QA Manager – Contamination Control Strategy 80-100% (f/m/d)

titleByLocale.it: QA Manager – Strategia di controllo della contaminazione 80-100% (f/m/d)

jobs.description:
Location: The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

You will own defined parts of the site Contamination Control Strategy at one of the largest biologics manufacturing sites in the world, where your decisions shape how a sustained microbial state of control is held shift after shift, batch after batch. You will work alongside colleagues from across the globe, in a site set in the Swiss Alps of the Valais.

What you will get :

• A place in an inclusive, dynamic, and passionate team.

• An unusually broad range of topics and disciplines to accelerate your professional growth and development.

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

What you will do:

• Own defined areas within the site Contamination Control Strategy and hold a sustained microbial state of control across facilities, equipment, processes, and supporting systems.

• Act as the technical lead for contamination control, hygiene, and microbiological QC topics in deviations, change controls, root cause investigations, impact assessments, and CAPAs, including their definition and follow-up.

• Ensure equipment, facility, and process hygienic integrity across the full lifecycle, from design and qualification through start-up, routine use, cleaning and sanitization, maintenance, and bioburden remediation.

• Provide visible shopfloor presence, hands-on technical support, day-to-day guidance, and leadership on contamination control, hygiene, and microbiological QC topics, including GEMBAs, oversight of microbiological methods and testing, and alignment of data trending practices with site contamination control requirements.

• Develop and improve hygiene concepts, gowning expectations, aseptic behavior, microbial hold times, bioburden and endotoxin control strategies, and the associated risk assessments.

• Represent CCS and microbiological quality topics in projects, audits, regulatory inspections, and cross-functional governance forums spanning QA, QC, MSAT, Engineering, Validations, and Operations.

• Drive continuous improvement, KPI delivery, training, and technical guidance commensurate with the risk profile of the site, and stay current with evolving regulatory expectations and industry best practice.

What we are looking for:

• Academic degree in Microbiology/ Biotechnology/ Pharmaceutical Sciences or another relevant scientific discipline.

• Significant experience in GMP-regulated pharmaceutical or biotech manufacturing, with strong exposure to contamination control, microbiological quality, or sterility assurance-related topics.

• Hands-on experience in contamination control strategy, microbial risk management, and contamination prevention in manufacturing environments is highly desired.

• A Quality Assurance background is preferred, particularly in roles involving contamination control, deviations, change controls, investigations, CAPAs, and inspection readiness.

• Good knowledge of GMP requirements and relevant regulatory expectations, including EU GMP Annex 1, EudraLex Volume 4, and applicable pharmacopoeial requirements and microbiological compendia.

• Understanding of key contamination control elements such as environmental and utilities monitoring, hygiene, gowning, aseptic practices, microbial control strategies, cleanroom behaviors, and qualified state maintenance.

• Strong communication and stakeholder-management skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across QA, QC, Engineering, Operations, and Microbiology.

• Fluency in English (written and spoken), German is an advantage.

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.
Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: HR Engagement & Communication Intern 80-100%
jobs.description:
We are seeking an enthusiastic and communicative intern who is eager to contribute to impactful HR communications at Lonza. This position offers a unique opportunity to gain hands‑on experience at the intersection of HR, communication, and organizational culture.

What you will do:

• Support HR Senior Management in preparing, structuring, and refining targeted HR communications for different internal audiences (e.g., HR community, leaders, employees)

• Summarize and translate HR strategies, initiatives, and outcomes into clear, concise, and engaging communication formats.

• Prepare and maintain HR-related content for internal platforms, in particular CoLab, ensuring information is up‑to‑date, easy to understand, and aligned with Lonza messaging, including review and approval by Group Internal Communications.

• Support external-facing HR communications, including LinkedIn posts and employer branding–related content, in close alignment with HR and Corporate Communications.

• Actively promote HR initiatives, programs, and engagement activities, helping to increase visibility, awareness, and participation across Lonza.

• Support the creation of presentations, newsletters, announcements, talking points, and briefing materials for HR leadership meetings, townhalls, and global forums, in alignment with Group Design guidelines and including review and final approval from the Brand & Design team.

• Coordinate timelines, inputs, and materials related to HR campaigns, engagement activities, and key people moments across the year.

• Review, edit, and ensure quality and consistency of HR communication materials (tone of voice, structure, branding).

• Provide general administrative and ad‑hoc support to the CHRO, the Head of HR Corporate Functions, and the wider HR leadership team as required.

•  Excellent written and spoken English; additional languages are an advantage.

What we are looking for:

• Studies (BA and/or Master) in Business Administration, Human Resources, Communications, Social Sciences, Marketing, or a related field.

• Strong interest in HR, internal communications, engagement, and organizational culture.

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English; ability to structure complex content into clear key messages.

• Good understanding of integrated communication, understanding stakeholders’ needs (what’s in it for them) and optimal channels for where/how to reach them.

• High proficiency in MS Office, especially PowerPoint (storytelling, slide design) andWord; experience with collaboration platforms (e.g., CoLab, SharePoint) is an advantage.

• Affinity for digital communication channels and social media, including LinkedIn.

• Proactive, reliable, and well‑organized working style, with the ability to handle multiple topics in parallel and to collaborate effectively in a global, matrix organization.  

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Bioprocess Engineer USP 80%-100% (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

Join our Manufacturing Operations team in Visp, where you will play a key role in delivering life‑changing biopharmaceuticals. As a Bioprocess Engineer USP, you will drive operational excellence in Downstream Processing, ensuring safe, compliant, and on‑time execution of manufacturing campaigns in a state‑of‑the‑art biotech environment.

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits.

Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

What you will do:

• Plan, execute, and oversee USP manufacturing campaigns, ensuring safety, quality standards, and on‑time‑in‑full delivery.

• Evaluate process performance, interpret test results, troubleshoot USP equipment, and recommend solutions to operational issues.

• Prepare and execute SHE, EM, operational, and cleaning risk assessments for assigned products.

• Author, review, and maintain GMP‑relevant documents, SOPs, tech transfer documentation, recipes, and EBRs.

• Prepare, review, and support quality records including deviations, change controls, CAPAs, and investigations.

• Perform or support qualification activities for equipment, manufacturing suites, and automation systems (MES/DeltaV).

• Lead USP operational readiness activities, including process validation, training, in‑process control, and other startup‑phase deliverables.

What we are looking for:

• A BSc or MSc in a relevant scientific or engineering discipline with proven experience in USP manufacturing, or a candidate without an academic degree but with 7+ years of hands‑on experience in USP operations or a similar role.

• Solid expertise in GMP environments, USP workflows, troubleshooting, and bioprocess equipment.

• Experience with MES/DeltaV recipe creation, execution, or review is a strong advantage.

• Structured and proactive way of working with the ability to collaborate effectively with cross‑functional teams.

• Strong communication skills and readiness to support customer audits.

• Ability to lead tasks independently and contribute to continuous improvement and operational excellence.

• Willingness to participate in on‑call duty/Pikett.

About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.
Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Internship Global Master Data Analyst 80-100% (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across five continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.

What you will do:

The Master Data department in Visp is a global team responsible for the development of master data processes and standards that are to be followed by the whole organization. As an intern, you will play an active role in:

• Perform various data analyses and reports to support Master data or Business projects 

• Assisting with SAP Master Data mass maintenance and migration efforts

• Gathering feedback from global and local Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) across the organization

• Defining functional and technical requirements for the development or enhancement of Master Data processes

• Updating and improving training materials, and delivering hands-on training sessions to teams across Lonza

As a part of the Global Master Data team, you will be able to learn SAP Materials Management (MM), Production & Planning (PP) and Sales & Distribution (SD) modules, key business processes as well as SAP data structures. Overall, you will interact with many different departments and experts across the company worldwide.

What we are looking for:

• A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree (or equivalent experience) in data science, computer science, business information technology, or mathematics

• Solid understanding of data structures or hands-on experience with databases

• Proficiency in English; German and other languages are a valuable plus

• A passion for optimizing processes, improving IT tools, and exploring innovative solutions

• You are interested in being a reliable team member in a multi-cultural environment.

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Equipment Expert Quality Control 80-100% (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
Lonza ist heute ein weltweit führendes Unternehmen im Bereich Life Sciences, welches auf drei Kontinenten tätig ist. Wir sind in der Wissenschaft tätig, jedoch gibt es keine Zauberformel dafür, wie wir die Arbeit angehen. Unsere wichtigste wissenschaftliche Lösung sind talentierte Mitarbeitende, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die anderen Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug steuern unsere Mitarbeitenden ihre Karriere selbstständig. Denn ihre Ideen, ob gross und klein, verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, an der wir Teil haben möchten. 

 

Am Standort Visp  wird ein/e Labor Equipment Expert- /in gesucht. Als Mitglied des QC Labor Support Teams übernehmen Sie unter Einhaltung der geltenden Qualitäts‑ und Sicherheitsrichtlinien vielfältige Aufgaben zur Sicherstellung eines reibungslosen Lifecycles von Laborequipment. Neben all den Routineaufgaben an den Geräten, erledigen Sie auch spontan auftretende Aufgaben. In Ihrer Funktion stehen Sie in regelmässigem Austausch mit verschiedenen Schnittstellen, unter anderem mit den Laboren, der Logistik sowie der Qualitätssicherung. 

Was wir ihnen bieten: 

• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur 

• Einen integrativen und ethischen Arbeitsplatz 

• Vergütungsprogramme, die hohe Leistung anerkennen 

• Neben wettbewerbsfähigen Gehältern finden Sie eine breite Palette an Lifestyle-, Familien- und Freizeitvorteilen. Je nach Standort können diese Vorteile Familienzulagen, Unterstützung bei der Kinderbetreuung, Rabatte für lokale Unternehmen und Attraktionen, Reisekostenzuschüsse und subventionierte Mahlzeiten umfassen. 

Ihre Aufgaben: 

• Sicherstellung des gesamten GMP Lifecycles von Geräten über Bestellung, Qualifizierung, Wartung, Reparatur, Requalifizierung bis hin zur Dekommissionierung der Geräte. 

• Organisation / Betreuung von externen und internen Servicetechnikern. 

• Bearbeitung aller nötigen Dokumente, welche für die Geräte relevant sind. 

• Mithilfe bei der Bearbeitung von Abweichungen / Trouble Shooting. 

• Abarbeitung von spontanen Anfragen aus dem Labor. 

 

Ihr Anforderungsprofil: 

• Sie verfügen über eine abgeschlossene Ausbildung, bevorzugt im Laborumfeld . 

• Sie sind selbstständig, verantwortungsbewusst und besitzen eine gute Kommunikations- und Teamfähigkeit sowie Freude an interdisziplinärer Zusammenarbeit. 

• Sie sind bereit, sich in ein Team zu integrieren, sind engagiert, motiviert und besitzen eine gute Eigeninitiative. 

• Arbeitserfahrung im GMP Umfeld wird bevorzugt 

• EDV Kenntnisse (Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams usw.) werden vorausgesetzt, Laborsoftwares wie LIMS sind ein Vorteil. 

• Sichere Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift. Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil. 

 

Lonza’s Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt.  

 

Zu Lonza kommen Menschen, die Herausforderungen annehmen und mit ihrer Kreativität im Bereich 

Life Science neue Ideen für komplexe Problemstellungen entwickeln. Zusammen leisten wir einen Beitrag, der das Leben vieler Menschen auf der ganzen Welt verbessern kann. Dies bietet ihnen die Gewissheit und die Zufriedenheit, mit der eigenen Arbeit wirklich einen Unterschied zu machen.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

Zusammen leisten wir einen Beitrag, der das Leben vieler Menschen auf der ganzen Welt verbessern kann. Dies bietet ihnen die Gewissheit und die Zufriedenheit, mit der eigenen Arbeit wirklich einen Unterschied zu machen. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Lonza ist heute ein weltweit führendes Unternehmen im Bereich Life Sciences, welches auf drei Kontinenten tätig ist. Wir sind in der Wissenschaft tätig, jedoch gibt es keine Zauberformel dafür, wie wir die Arbeit angehen. Unsere wichtigste wissenschaftliche Lösung sind talentierte Mitarbeitende, die zusammenarbeiten und Ideen entwickeln, die anderen Unternehmen helfen, Menschen zu helfen. Im Gegenzug steuern unsere Mitarbeitenden ihre Karriere selbstständig. Denn ihre Ideen, ob gross und klein, verbessern die Welt. Und das ist die Art von Arbeit, an der wir Teil haben möchten. Am Standort Visp wird ein/e Labor Equipment Expert- /in gesucht. Als Mitglied des QC Labor Support Teams übernehmen Sie unter Einhaltung der geltenden Qualitäts‑ und Sicherheitsrichtlinien vielfältige Aufgaben zur Sicherstellung eines reibungslosen Lifecycles von Laborequipment. Neben all den Routineaufgaben an den Geräten, erledigen Sie auch spontan auftretende Aufgaben. In Ihrer Funktion stehen Sie in regelmässigem Austausch mit verschiedenen Schnittstellen, unter anderem mit den Laboren, der Logistik sowie der Qualitätssicherung. Was wir ihnen bieten:
• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur
• Einen integrativen und ethischen Arbeitsplatz
• Vergütungsprogramme, die hohe Leistung anerkennen
• Neben wettbewerbsfähigen Gehältern finden Sie eine breite Palette an Lifestyle-, Familien- und Freizeitvorteilen. Je nach Standort können diese Vorteile Familienzulagen, Unterstützung bei der Kinderbetreuung, Rabatte für lokale Unternehmen und Attraktionen, Reisekostenzuschüsse und subventionierte Mahlzeiten umfassen. Ihre Aufgaben:
• Sicherstellung des gesamten GMP Lifecycles von Geräten über Bestellung, Qualifizierung, Wartung, Reparatur, Requalifizierung bis hin zur Dekommissionierung der Geräte.
• Organisation / Betreuung von externen und internen Servicetechnikern.
• Bearbeitung aller nötigen Dokumente, welche für die Geräte relevant sind.
• Mithilfe bei der Bearbeitung von Abweichungen / Trouble Shooting.
• Abarbeitung von spontanen Anfragen aus dem Labor. Ihr Anforderungsprofil:
• Sie verfügen über eine abgeschlossene Ausbildung, bevorzugt im Laborumfeld .
• Sie sind selbstständig, verantwortungsbewusst und besitzen eine gute Kommunikations- und Teamfähigkeit sowie Freude an interdisziplinärer Zusammenarbeit.
• Sie sind bereit, sich in ein Team zu integrieren, sind engagiert, motiviert und besitzen eine gute Eigeninitiative.
• Arbeitserfahrung im GMP Umfeld wird bevorzugt
• EDV Kenntnisse (Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams usw.) werden vorausgesetzt, Laborsoftwares wie LIMS sind ein Vorteil.
• Sichere Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift. Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil. Lonza’s Produkte und Services wirken sich täglich positiv auf Millionen von Menschen aus. Für uns stellt dies nicht nur eine grosse Ehre, sondern auch eine grosse Verantwortung, dar. Wie wir unsere Geschäftsergebnisse erreichen ist für uns genauso wichtig wie unsere Erfolge selbst. Bei Lonza wird Respekt grossgeschrieben und wir schützen sowohl unsere Mitarbeiter als auch unsere Umwelt. Erfolg für uns bedeutet moralisch vertretbarer Fortschritt. Zu Lonza kommen Menschen, die Herausforderungen annehmen und mit ihrer Kreativität im Bereich Life Science neue Ideen für komplexe Problemstellungen entwickeln. Zusammen leisten wir einen Beitrag, der das Leben vieler Menschen auf der ganzen Welt verbessern kann. Dies bietet ihnen die Gewissheit und die Zufriedenheit, mit der eigenen Arbeit wirklich einen Unterschied zu machen. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Bioprocess Engineer in USP / DSP / BioConjugates

titleByLocale.it: Bioprocess Ingegnere in USP / DSP / BioConjugates
titleByLocale.en: Bioprocess Engineer in USP / DSP / BioConjugates
titleByLocale.de: Bioprocess Ingenieur in USP / DSP / BioConjugates
titleByLocale.fr: Bioprocess Ingénieur in USP / DSP / BioConjugates

jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is dedicated people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.

As the global manufacturing partner for many different customers, we are increasing manufacturing capacity. As other businesses face increasing economic uncertainty, we are fortunate to have opportunities for expansion. Through this new collaboration, our work will have a significant, direct and long-term impact on the management of this global pandemic.
In order to support our continued growth Lonza is seeking Bioprocess Engineers with a relevant background in Life Sciences and a passion for Biotechnology to join our talent community in Visp, Switzerland. Become part of this great opportunity and join our team by applying for the position as Bioprocess Engineer in Upstream Processing or Downstream Processing or BioConjugate Manufacturing.

Key responsibilities:

• Ensure that operational activities for clinical and/or commercial manufacturing are performed on-time and within given safety and quality standards

• Act as the functional lead for preparation, manufacturing execution, cleaning and changeover in the assigned area

• Lead of one or more defined USP areas (e.g. seed train, main cultivation or harvest) for the production of therapeutic proteins with either mammalian or microbial expressions systems (Bioprocess Engineer USP)

• In charge of one or more defined DSP areas (e.g. gain, polishing or filling) for the purification of therapeutic proteins from either mammalian or microbial cells (Bioprocess Engineer DSP)

• Responsible for entire processes or process units for clinical and commercial manufacturing projects in therapeutic BioConjugates like Antibody Drug Conjugates (ADC) (Bioprocess Engineer BioConjugates)

Key requirements:

• Master’s Degree or equivalent experience in Biotechnology, Biochemical Engineering, Biochemistry or related Life Science subject, or Bachelor’s holder with experience in pharma production

• Knowledge in either USP (such as bioreactors) or DSP units (such as chromatography)

• Working experience in biomanufacturing would be an advantage

• GMP experience and understanding of bioprocess technology

• Excellent communication skills in English, good German skills of advantage

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.

People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a significant difference.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Manufacturing Specialist Ibex® 100% (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is dedicated people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.

Ibex® Solutions (www.ibex.lonza.com) is a modular build complex to develop and manufacture biological products. It enables companies to get access to a complete solution, gaining speed and achieving a simplified value chain.
The Ibex® Complex is our contribution to the medicine of tomorrow and possibly the next step in your career?
 
At our location in Visp, in the canton of Valais, we are constantly looking for Biotechnologists with a relevant background at all levels in Life Sciences in order to support our growth in several business units. 

Key responsibilities:

• Assignment in various functions within the production facilities in compliance with safety regulations and consideration of cGMP quality requirements. 

• Recognition, interpretation and elimination of deviations

• Execution of production steps incl. recording and calculations

• Support in training of new team members

Key requirements:

• Completed chemical / biopharmaceutical education

• Professional experience in pharmaceutical production as an operator/production technician etc

• Safety-conscious work

• Knowledge of process engineering

• Fluent English

• German language skills advantageous 

• Willingness to work in shifts (e.g. 4-shift pattern) with flexible work scheduling

 
Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. jobs.title: Manufacturing Specialist Mammalian 100% (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is dedicated people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.

Due to the growth at our site in Visp, we are currently looking for several Manufacturing Specialists for our Ibex-facility. In this role, you will be responsible for performing operational activities of assigned manufacturing projects in specific areas (Up-stream or Down-stream processing).

Key responsibilities:

• Complete manufacturing activities in either the Upstream Processing or Downstream Processing area according to cGMP guidelines, ensuring batch execution, evaluating test results, resolving issues, troubleshoot manufacturing equipment and make recommendations for resolution

• Support the preparation of SHE (Safety, Health, environment), EM (Environmental Monitoring), operation and cleaning risk assessment for the assigned products and the implementation and control of defined measures

• Assist document preparation (e.g. electronic batch records, SOPs) specific to the assigned production area

• Review production documentation and work on deviations, change requests and implementation of CAPAs to ensure high-quality GMP standards

• Engage in Capital investment projects for building and commissioning of new manufacturing units as manufacturing expert. Responsible for delivering all input required for the specific set-up, commissioning and qualification of the manufacturing suites and equipment 

• Responsible for the training of the manufacturing staff on specific equipment or standard operations

Key requirements:

• Completed chemical / biopharmaceutical education or corresponding training qualification, Bachelor / Master's degree or equivalent experience in Biotechnology, Biology, Biochemical Engineering or related fields is an advantage

• Sound knowledge of biotechnological processes either in the area of microbial and/or mammalian cultivation or protein purification

• Excellent communication skills, great teammate, driven and solution-oriented

• Fluency in English required, fluency in German is considered a plus

• Open to work in shift (4-shift system)

• Basic GMP understanding is a plus

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as meaningful as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically. 
 
People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a significant difference.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Biotechnologist 100% (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across five continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is dedicated people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of.
At our location in Visp, in the canton of Valais, we are constantly looking for Biotechnologists with a relevant background at all levels in Life Sciences in order to support our growth in several business units!

The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

What you’ll do:

• Assignment in various functions within the production facilities in compliance with safety regulations and consideration of cGMP quality requirements.

• Recognition, interpretation and elimination of deviations

• Execution of production steps incl. recording and calculations

• Support in training of new team members

What we’re looking for:

• Completed chemical / biopharmaceutical education

• Professional experience in pharmaceutical production as an operator/production technician etc

• Safety-conscious work

• Knowledge of process engineering

• Fluent English with German language skills advantageous

• Willingness to work in shifts (e.g. 4-shift pattern) with flexible work scheduling

Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.

People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a significant difference.

Lonza is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a qualified individual with disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Project Lead Phys-Chem QC 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Basel Stücki, Switzerland.

For our successful Drug Product focused branch located in Basel and Stein, Switzerland, we have exciting opportunities. Become part of a group that enables its customers to meet some of the greatest challenges in patient treatment and join us by applying for the R&D Project Lead position.

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture

• An inclusive and ethical workplace

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance

What you will do:

• Lead pharmaceutical development projects from early phases to market readiness

• Ensure timely delivery of QC work-packages and documentation

• Represent QC in internal and customer meetings

• Address risks proactively and communicate effectively

• Maintain compliance with cGMP and SOPs

• Support audits and act as SME for QC topics

• Drive process improvements and share best practices

What we are looking for:

• MSc or PhD in Analytical Chemistry, Biochemistry, Pharmaceutical Sciences or related field

• Experience in Analytics or Quality Control under cGMP regulations

• Knowledge of analytical lifecycle for biotechnology products and CMC requirements

• Strong communication and interpersonal skills with a proactive attitude

• Detail-oriented and able to work in a matrix organization

• Business fluent English, written and spoken

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: MSAT Process Expert Mammalian Upstream Large-Scale (m/f/d)

titleByLocale.it: MSAT Process Expert Mammifero a monte su larga scala (m/f/d)
titleByLocale.en: MSAT Process Expert Mammalian Upstream Large-Scale (m/f/d)
titleByLocale.de: MSAT Process Expert Mammalian Upstream Large-Scale (m/f/d)
titleByLocale.fr: MSAT Process Expert Mammalian Upstream Large-Scale (m/f/d)

jobs.description:
Today, Lonza is a global leader in life sciences operating across three continents. While we work in science, there’s no magic formula to how we do it. Our greatest scientific solution is talented people working together, devising ideas that help businesses to help people. In exchange, we let our people own their careers. Their ideas, big and small, genuinely improve the world. And that’s the kind of work we want to be part of. 
 
The site in Visp, Switzerland is growing and for our Biologics organization, we are currently looking for experienced Process Experts and Senior Process Experts in Ibex® Solutions, our recently launched unique biological manufacturing and development concept. We have opportunities in both USP and DSP in Drug Substance for mammalian large-scale manufacturing (up to 20.000L). As a process owner, you are responsible for the successful scale up, supervision and optimization of mammalian processes. Become part of this great opportunity and apply now!
 
Key responsibilities:

• You lead multi-functional teams tasked with technology transfers (process design and up-scaling) and timely process implementation at manufacturing scale

• You act as the interface between the process donor (customer, process development) and manufacturing

• You directly connect with customers during campaign preparation, execution and closure including regular reporting of batch status and process performance

• You are responsible for the resolution of process issues that may arise during manufacturing considering all regulatory requirements

• You secure technology transfer success by applying standardized processes and tools to manage the transfer of information, process related risks and change control

 
Key requirements:

• Bachelor, Master's degree or PhD or equivalent experience in Biotechnology, chemical engineering or related field

• Significant experience in the area of biopharma manufacturing and / or process development preferably in Mammalian Manufacturing (large-scale)

• GMP experience and understanding of bioprocess technology

• Excellent communication skills in English and/or German

 
Every day, Lonza’s products and services have a positive impact on millions of people. For us, this is not only a great privilege, but also a great responsibility. How we achieve our business results is just as important as the achievements themselves. At Lonza, we respect and protect our people and our environment. Any success we achieve is no success at all if not achieved ethically.
 
People come to Lonza for the challenge and creativity of solving complex problems and developing new ideas in life sciences. In return, we offer the satisfaction that comes with improving lives all around the world. The satisfaction that comes with making a meaningful difference.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Manufacturing Line Specialist - Anlagemeister (AM) (m/w/d) 80%-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

In the role of the Manufacturing Line Specialist - Anlagemeister (AM) you ensure through intensive technical and organizational support of production campaigns in a 6x20k multipurpose, mammalian large-scale facility, the manufacturing of therapeutic proteins for our commercial customers while adhering to HSE, hygiene, and cGMP guidelines.

What you will get: 

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi 

What you will do:

• Equipment availability – this includes HSE, tidiness, cleanliness and coordination of maintenance & calibration of state-of-the-art equipment in collaboration with internal technical stakeholders. Serve as first point of contact in equipment malfunctions and lead the troubleshooting in close collaboration with the Bioprocess Engineers.

• Training - Ensuring that employees assigned to a project or equipment have a good level of training regarding cGMP, HSE, hygiene, and technical competencies.

• Cross-functional - Support the manufacturing of therapeutic products through risk analyses, equipment modification, technical optimizations and coordination of routine technical tasks.

• Culture - Implements the One-Lonza vision and culture and supports its further development through openness to change, new ideas, and continuous improvements.

What we are looking for:

• Completed apprenticeship as a biotechnological/pharmaceutical/chemical technologist (chemical technician) or B.Sc./M.Sc. degree in technical or scientific field with relevant work experience in large-scale biotechnological plants.

• You demonstrate high flexibility, teamwork ability, organizational skills, and strong commitment.

• Fluency in English (spoken/written), German is considered a plus.

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance. In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.
• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi What you will do:
• Equipment availability – this includes HSE, tidiness, cleanliness and coordination of maintenance & calibration of state-of-the-art equipment in collaboration with internal technical stakeholders. Serve as first point of contact in equipment malfunctions and lead the troubleshooting in close collaboration with the Bioprocess Engineers.
• Training - Ensuring that employees assigned to a project or equipment have a good level of training regarding cGMP, HSE, hygiene, and technical competencies.
• Cross-functional - Support the manufacturing of therapeutic products through risk analyses, equipment modification, technical optimizations and coordination of routine technical tasks.
• Culture - Implements the One-Lonza vision and culture and supports its further development through openness to change, new ideas, and continuous improvements. What we are looking for:
• Completed apprenticeship as a biotechnological/pharmaceutical/chemical technologist (chemical technician) or B.Sc./M.Sc. degree in technical or scientific field with relevant work experience in large-scale biotechnological plants.
• You demonstrate high flexibility, teamwork ability, organizational skills, and strong commitment.
• Fluency in English (spoken/written), German is considered a plus. About Lonza At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. jobs.title: Supply Chain Lead DP Development Services (f/m/d)

titleByLocale.it: Supply Chain Lead DP Development Services (f/m/d)
titleByLocale.en: Supply Chain Lead DP Development Services (f/m/d)
titleByLocale.fr: Chaîne d'approvisionnement Lead DP Development Services (f/m/d)

jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Basel Stücki, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

For Drug Product Services (DPS), our successful DP focused branch located in Basel, Switzerland, we have exciting opportunities!  Become part of a group that enables its customers to meet some of the greatest challenges in patient treatment. Are you ready for your next challenge? Apply now and join this outstanding team as Supply Chain Lead DPS Development Services!

The Supply Chain Lead DPS Development Services is accountable for consistent and flawless supply chain execution at the site level. On that purpose, the person is translating customers’ demand into executable schedules for which material, asset and resources are available and develops and sets practices in alignment with the Lonza global supply chain. The role is also responsible for operational buying of materials and warehouse/ logistics operations.

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture

• An inclusive and ethical workplace

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance

What you will do:

• SC Team management including optimal resource's allocation, hiring, gap analysis and talent development

• Flawless coordination of site planning alignment (from demand to execution)

• Ensuring through the warehouse/ logistics team that the movement of material across the site and distribution of products to the customers is conducted

• All medium- and short- term planning processes to ensure fully integrated asset capacity planning, labor planning and material planning (monthly S&OP, weekly operations planning and daily tier process)

• Lead the local monthly S&OP meeting, provide a monthly forecast of all site-based deliverables, supports Finance/Controlling/Program Management financial outlook for the site as agreed at the meeting and submit to Platform Planner

• Robust sourcing information operational buying activities

• Oversee Inventory Management and Materials Flows

• Accountable for Planning and Material Master Data and as well SAP Blueprint implementation, execution and certification

What we are looking for:

• Degree or equivalent experience in Life Sciences with solid knowledge in APICS CPIM professional standards

• Over 10 years of work experience in
a) Laboratory Planning and execution – in a testing or development lab and
b) Supply Chain / Logistics - in a manufacturing organization

• Strong SAP background with skills in financial, scheduling and materials planning is a must (Planview background an additional benefit)

• Very strong leadership skills with a proven ability to motivate and manage teams not only in a traditional hierarchical organizational structure and but also enable teams in a matrix structure

• Demonstrates the courage and integrity to engage in difficult conversations with internal teams and management when needed, fostering transparency, growth, and collaboration

• Ability to build a highly collaborative and inclusive environment necessary for a team to be effective

• Strong communication and presentation skills, ability to present ideas in a clear and concise manner

• Fluent in English, German is a strong asset

About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Operator Bioconjugates (m/w/d)
jobs.description:
Der Standort dieser Position ist in Visp, Schweiz. Bei Bedarf steht für berechtigte Kandidaten und ihre Familien Unterstützung beim Umzug zur Verfügung.

Was wir Ihnen bieten:

• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur

• Ein inklusiver und ethischer Arbeitsplatz

• Vergütungsprogramme, die hohe Leistung anerkennen

Zusätzlich zu einem wettbewerbsfähigen Gehalt können Sie zahlreiche Vorteile für Lebensstil, Familie und Freizeit erwarten. Die vollständige Liste unserer lokal zugeschnittenen Benefits finden Sie unten.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

Ihre Aufgaben:

• Selbstständiger Betrieb vertrauter Teile der Produktionsanlage gemäß Spezifikationsdokumenten (Herstellprotokolle, Arbeitsanweisungen, Betriebsanweisungen, SOPs)

• Sichere und präzise Durchführung aller Arbeiten unter Einhaltung der Sicherheitsvorschriften und cGMP-Qualitätsanforderungen

• Verantwortung für Sauberkeit und Ordnung am Arbeitsplatz sowie in den zugewiesenen Räumen und Lagerbereichen

• Aktive Mitarbeit als gleichwertiges Teammitglied in kontinuierlichen Verbesserungsprozessen innerhalb der Ibex®-Operationen

Ihr Profil

• Abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung (EFZ) oder Ausbildung im Bereich Chemie-, Biologie- oder Pharmatechnologie – oder nachweisbare relevante Berufserfahrung

• Sorgfältige und genaue Arbeitsweise sowie starke Dokumentationsfähigkeiten

• Hohes Sicherheitsbewusstsein im täglichen Arbeiten

• Bereitschaft zur Arbeit im 4-Schichtbetrieb sowie Flexibilität in der Arbeitsplanung

• Kenntnisse in DeltaV oder MES sowie Erfahrung im GMP-Umfeld sind von Vorteil

• Mindestens eine der folgenden Sprachen auf Niveau B2: Deutsch oder Englisch; die andere Sprache mindestens Niveau B1.

• Bereitschaft zur Arbeit im 4-Schichtbetrieb

Bei Lonza sind unsere Mitarbeitenden unsere grösste Stärke. Mit über 30 Standorten auf fünf Kontinenten arbeiten unsere global vernetzten Teams jeden Tag zusammen, um die Medikamente von morgen herzustellen. Unsere Kernwerte – Zusammenarbeit, Verantwortlichkeit, Exzellenz, Leidenschaft und Integrität – spiegeln wider, wer wir sind und wie wir zusammenarbeiten. Jede Idee, ob gross oder klein, hat das Potenzial, Millionen von Leben zu verbessern – und genau an dieser Arbeit möchten wir Sie beteiligen.

Innovation gedeiht, wenn Menschen aus unterschiedlichen Hintergründen ihre einzigartigen Perspektiven einbringen. Bei Lonza schätzen wir Vielfalt und setzen uns für ein inklusives Umfeld für alle Mitarbeitenden ein. Wenn Sie bereit sind, die bahnbrechenden Ideen unserer Kunden in umsetzbare Therapien zu verwandeln, freuen wir uns darauf, Sie an Bord zu begrüssen.

Bereit, die Zukunft der Life Sciences mitzugestalten? Bewerben Sie sich jetzt.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

Jede Idee, ob gross oder klein, hat das Potenzial, Millionen von Leben zu verbessern – und genau an dieser Arbeit möchten wir Sie beteiligen. Innovation gedeiht, wenn Menschen aus unterschiedlichen Hintergründen ihre einzigartigen Perspektiven einbringen. Bei Lonza schätzen wir Vielfalt und setzen uns für ein inklusives Umfeld für alle Mitarbeitenden ein. Wenn Sie bereit sind, die bahnbrechenden Ideen unserer Kunden in umsetzbare Therapien zu verwandeln, freuen wir uns darauf, Sie an Bord zu begrüssen. Bereit, die Zukunft der Life Sciences mitzugestalten? Bewerben Sie sich jetzt. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Biotechnologist (m/f/d)
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

What you will get:

• An agile career and a dynamic work culture.

• An inclusive and ethical workplace.

• Compensation programs that recognize high performance.

In addition to a competitive salary, you can expect numerous lifestyle, family, and leisure benefits. Our full list of tailored local benefits can be found below.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi 

What you will do:

• Independent operation and execution of downstream related activities according to specified instructions (manufacturing protocols / operating instructions / work instructions / SOP)

• Safe and correct execution of work in compliance with safety regulations, taking into account the cGMP quality requirements

• Responsibility for cleanliness and order at the workplace as well as the assigned rooms and storage areas

• Performing monitoring, maintenance, and cleaning tasks, as well as reporting and rectifying defects and malfunctions in the facility, including their documentation

• Every employee is involved as an equal team member through their active participation in continuous improvement processes

What we are looking for:

• Completed professional training (Apprenticeship)

• Chemical or pharmaceutical-technical training as well as GMP and/or BICO training advantageous

• Good skills in speaking, writing, and comprehension in German or English required.

• Accurate work and documentation practices

• Execution of tasks with a high level of safety and quality awareness

• Willingness to work shifts (4-shift operation) with flexibility in work scheduling

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

German Version:
Der Standort dieser Position ist in Visp, Schweiz. Bei Bedarf steht für berechtigte Kandidaten und ihre Familien Unterstützung beim Umzug zur Verfügung.

Was wir Ihnen bieten:

• Eine agile Karriere und eine dynamische Arbeitskultur

• Ein inklusiver und ethischer Arbeitsplatz

• Vergütungsprogramme, die hohe Leistung anerkennen

Zusätzlich zu einem wettbewerbsfähigen Gehalt können Sie zahlreiche Vorteile für Lebensstil, Familie und Freizeit erwarten. Die vollständige Liste unserer lokal zugeschnittenen Benefits finden Sie unten.

• Benefits in Visp: https://bit.ly/3wjkoFi

Ihre Aufgaben:

• Selbstständige Durchführung und Ausführung von nachgelagerten Tätigkeiten gemäß vorgegebenen Anweisungen (Herstellungsprotokolle / Bedienungsanweisungen / Arbeitsanweisungen / SOPs)

• Sicheres und korrektes Ausführen der Arbeiten unter Einhaltung der Sicherheitsvorschriften und unter Berücksichtigung der cGMP-Qualitätsanforderungen

• Verantwortung für Sauberkeit und Ordnung am Arbeitsplatz sowie in den zugewiesenen Räumen und Lagerbereichen

• Durchführung von Überwachungs-, Wartungs- und Reinigungsarbeiten sowie Meldung und Behebung von Mängeln und Störungen an den Anlagen inklusive deren Dokumentation

• Aktive Mitwirkung aller Mitarbeitenden als gleichwertige Teammitglieder an kontinuierlichen Verbesserungsprozessen

 Ihr Anforderungsprofil:

• Abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung (Lehre)

• Chemische oder pharmazeutisch-technische Ausbildung sowie GMP- und/oder BICO-Kenntnisse von Vorteil

• Gute Kenntnisse in Wort und Schrift in Deutsch oder Englisch erforderlich

• Sorgfältige Arbeitsweise und gewissenhafte Dokumentation

• Hohes Sicherheits- und Qualitätsbewusstsein bei der Ausführung der Aufgaben

• Bereitschaft zur Schichtarbeit (4-Schicht-Betrieb) mit Flexibilität in der Arbeitszeitgestaltung

Bei Lonza sind unsere Mitarbeitenden unsere grösste Stärke. Mit über 30 Standorten auf fünf Kontinenten arbeiten unsere global vernetzten Teams jeden Tag zusammen, um die Medikamente von morgen herzustellen. Unsere Kernwerte – Zusammenarbeit, Verantwortlichkeit, Exzellenz, Leidenschaft und Integrität – spiegeln wider, wer wir sind und wie wir zusammenarbeiten. Jede Idee, ob gross oder klein, hat das Potenzial, Millionen von Leben zu verbessern – und genau an dieser Arbeit möchten wir Sie beteiligen.

Innovation gedeiht, wenn Menschen aus unterschiedlichen Hintergründen ihre einzigartigen Perspektiven einbringen. Bei Lonza schätzen wir Vielfalt und setzen uns für ein inklusives Umfeld für alle Mitarbeitenden ein. Wenn Sie bereit sind, die bahnbrechenden Ideen unserer Kunden in umsetzbare Therapien zu verwandeln, freuen wir uns darauf, Sie an Bord zu begrüssen.

Bereit, die Zukunft der Life Sciences mitzugestalten? Bewerben Sie sich jetzt.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

• Responsibility for cleanliness and order at the workplace as well as the assigned rooms and storage areas
• Performing monitoring, maintenance, and cleaning tasks, as well as reporting and rectifying defects and malfunctions in the facility, including their documentation
• Every employee is involved as an equal team member through their active participation in continuous improvement processes What we are looking for:
• Completed professional training (Apprenticeship)
• Chemical or pharmaceutical-technical training as well as GMP and/or BICO training advantageous
• Good skills in speaking, writing, and comprehension in German or English required.
• Accurate work and documentation practices
• Execution of tasks with a high level of safety and quality awareness
• Willingness to work shifts (4-shift operation) with flexibility in work scheduling At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board. Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now. • Sicheres und korrektes Ausführen der Arbeiten unter Einhaltung der Sicherheitsvorschriften und unter Berücksichtigung der cGMP-Qualitätsanforderungen

jobs.title: Scientist (m/f/d) 80-100%
jobs.description:
The actual location of this job is in Visp, Switzerland. Relocation assistance is available for eligible candidates and their families, if needed.

​We are currently looking for a scientist (m/f/d) to join our process development team for   bioconjugates in Visp, Switzerland. In our team, you are responsible for the planning and execution of laboratory experiments and you collaborate closely with project teams.  

​We are looking for you if you are a great team player, have the necessary skillset, and like contributing to bring bioconjugate-based biopharmaceuticals to the patient! 

What you will get:

• An agile career and dynamic working culture

• A highly collaborative environment that fosters teamwork and inclusion

• A range of professional development programs to grow your skills and career

• An ethical workplace where tasks are carried out in accordance to the relevant procedures

What you will do:

• Plan, perform and document laboratory experiments 

• ​Evaluate data for the preparation of reports or presentations

• Provide scientific support to lab technicians to ensure scientifically sound execution of experiments

• Support project leaders in the planning and organization of projects and experiments 

What we are looking for:

• ​BSc. or MSc. degree in chemistry, biotechnology or a related field  

• ​Good understanding of general laboratory routines

• ​Experience in protein handling, protein chromatography, filtration, and TFF is a plus 

• Sense of accuracy, quality, and initiative

• Very flexible, reliable, and proactive with strong team work skills

• Structured, focused and well-organized working attitude; open-minded for new ideas and suggestions; agile, highly motivated and dynamic; solution-oriented 

• Good written and oral communication skills in German and English. 

About Lonza

At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

jobs.title: Project Support Intern 80-100%
jobs.description:
The Project Support Intern at Lonza AG will assist project teams with administrative tasks, data management, and coordination to ensure the smooth execution of projects within our dynamic life sciences environment. This role provides an excellent opportunity to gain hands-on experience in project management support within a leading global company.

What you will get:

• A dynamic, agile career path with room to grow 

• An inclusive, ethical, and supportive workplace culture 

• A comprehensive benefits package tailored to your role and location

What you will do:

• Provide administrative support to project managers and project teams, including scheduling meetings, preparing agendas, and taking minutes.

• Assist in the maintenance of project documentation, including filing, archiving, and ensuring accessibility of relevant materials.

• Support data entry and management within project management systems and tools.

• Facilitate communication among project stakeholders, ensuring timely dissemination of information.

• Assist with the preparation of project reports, presentations, and other project-related deliverables.

• Support the organization of project workshops, events, and training sessions.

• Perform general office duties as required to support the project team.

• Adhere to all company policies, procedures, and safety guidelines.

What we are looking for:

• Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

• Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.

• Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).

• Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment.

• Proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and take initiative.

• Previous experience in an office environment or with project work is a plus but not required.

About Lonza
At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of.

Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now.

Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.

• Adhere to all company policies, procedures, and safety guidelines. What we are looking for:
• Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
• Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
• Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment.
• Proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and take initiative.
• Previous experience in an office environment or with project work is a plus but not required. About Lonza At Lonza, our people are our greatest strength. With 30+ sites across five continents, our globally connected teams work together every day to manufacture the medicines of tomorrow. Our core values of Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion and Integrity reflect who we are and how we work together. Everyone’s ideas, big or small, have the potential to improve millions of lives, and that’s the kind of work we want you to be part of. Innovation thrives when people from all backgrounds bring their unique perspectives to the table. At Lonza, we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you’re ready to help turn our customers’ breakthrough ideas into viable therapies, we look forward to welcoming you on board. Ready to shape the future of life sciences? Apply now. Lonza is a global leader in pharma and biotech manufacturing. The company operates major production facilities in Visp (Valais), Basel, and Stein (Aargau), Switzerland.
